# Extractor Receipt

## Summary

This source contains empirical evidence about how public Claude Code configuration files are collected, filtered, analyzed, and patterned, plus measured frequencies for common configuration concerns, auxiliary elements such as code examples and links, and stated threats to validity.

## Evidence Files

### file: evidence-01.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-029
evidence_id: evidence-01
kind: method
citation: Santos et al., arXiv 2511.09268, Abstract
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2511.09268
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The study analyzed 328 configuration files from public Claude Code projects.

## Source Excerpt

"This paper presents an empirical study of the configuration ecosystem of Claude Code... We collected and analyzed 328 configuration files from public Claude Code projects"

## Note

The source describes the dataset at the file level, not at the repository level.
```

### file: evidence-02.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-029
evidence_id: evidence-02
kind: method
citation: Santos et al., arXiv 2511.09268, Sec. 3.1
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2511.09268
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The initial GitHub Search API sweep found 4,724 repositories containing Claude.md between August 28 and August 30, 2025.

## Source Excerpt

"We first collected an initial list of GitHub repositories which are using Claude Code between August 28, 2025 and August 30, 2025... This initial search returned 4,724 repositories."

## Note

This count is for the initial repository detection stage before filtering.
```

### file: evidence-03.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-029
evidence_id: evidence-03
kind: method
citation: Santos et al., arXiv 2511.09268, Sec. 3.1
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2511.09268
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
Repository selection excluded projects with fewer than 100 stars, non-English repositories, and repositories judged not to be real-world applications, then kept the top 100 most popular remaining repositories.

## Source Excerpt

"We removed repositories based on the following criteria: (1) Popularity. We filtered out repositories with less than 100 stars... (2) Language. We excluded non-English repositories... (3) Actual Applications. We removed repositories that are not real-world applications... From the remaining ones, we selected the top-100 most popular repositories"

## Note

The real-world application filter was applied manually from repository description and README content.
```

### file: evidence-04.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-029
evidence_id: evidence-04
kind: metric
citation: Santos et al., arXiv 2511.09268, Sec. 3.1
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2511.09268
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The selected projects spanned 23 programming languages, with JavaScript/TypeScript in 35 projects, Python in 16, and Go in 9.

## Source Excerpt

"Overall, the projects are implemented in 23 programming languages, with JavaScript/TypeScript (35 projects), Python (16 projects), and Go (9 projects) being the most common ones."

## Note

The source reports language counts for projects, not for individual configuration files.
```

### file: evidence-05.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-029
evidence_id: evidence-05
kind: metric
citation: Santos et al., arXiv 2511.09268, Sec. 3.1
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2511.09268
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The selected repositories had a median of 950 stars, 488 commits, and 58 months since first commit.

## Source Excerpt

"All projects are fairly popular (median of 950 stars) and active (median of 488 commits). They are also long-lived, with a median age of 58 months since their first commit."

## Note

These are median repository-level characteristics for the selected sample.
```

### file: evidence-06.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-029
evidence_id: evidence-06
kind: method
citation: Santos et al., arXiv 2511.09268, Sec. 3.1
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2511.09268
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
During file fetching, the authors found 45 memory-bank files that only pointed to other markdown files and replaced them with the referenced contents, yielding 328 Claude.md files in total.

## Source Excerpt

"During this process, we detected 45 files functioning as memory banks, i.e., files that only pointed to other markdown files within their respective projects. In such cases, we retrieved the contents of the referenced files instead. At the end of this process, we obtained a total of 328 CLAUDE.md files."

## Note

The source treats pointed-to markdown as the analyzable content when a Claude.md file acts only as a pointer.
```

### file: evidence-07.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-029
evidence_id: evidence-07
kind: metric
citation: Santos et al., arXiv 2511.09268, Sec. 3.2, Table 1
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2511.09268
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
Claude.md files had a median of seven level-2 headings, a maximum of 213, and 36 files had no level-2 sections.

## Source Excerpt

"As we can see, the files have a median of seven level-2 headings. However, there is also one file with 213 headings. Interestingly, we found that 36 files have no level-2 sections at all."

## Note

The file-size proxy here is the number of markdown level-2 sections.
```

### file: evidence-08.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-029
evidence_id: evidence-08
kind: method
citation: Santos et al., arXiv 2511.09268, Sec. 3.2
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2511.09268
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
For files without level-2 sections, the authors analyzed level-1 headings instead.

## Source Excerpt

"After a manual analysis, we concluded that these files describe practices and rules directly at level 1 (# tag). Thus, in such cases, we decided to analyze the content of level-1 headings instead."

## Note

This is an explicit exception to the default focus on level-2 headings.
```

### file: evidence-09.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-029
evidence_id: evidence-09
kind: method
citation: Santos et al., arXiv 2511.09268, Sec. 3.2
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2511.09268
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The authors extracted 2,492 section titles and grouped them into semantically related categories through manual analysis by one author, then reviewed the classification with two other authors.

## Source Excerpt

"After following the previous procedures, we extracted 2,492 section titles. These sections were then manually analyzed by one of the authors, who grouped them into semantically related categories... Next, a meeting was held with two other authors, who inspected the proposed classification and confirmed it."

## Note

The categorization process combined single-author coding with multi-author review.
```

### file: evidence-10.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-029
evidence_id: evidence-10
kind: method
citation: Santos et al., arXiv 2511.09268, Sec. 3.3
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2511.09268
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
Pattern detection used the FP-Max algorithm from MLxtend with minimum support set to 0.15.

## Source Excerpt

"We used the FP-Max algorithm to detect which concerns and practices are mentioned together... We used the FP-Max implementation provided by the MLxtend library... configured with 0.15 minimum support."

## Note

Each Claude.md file was represented as a transaction of the concerns and practices it contains.
```

### file: evidence-11.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-029
evidence_id: evidence-11
kind: result
citation: Santos et al., arXiv 2511.09268, Sec. 4
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2511.09268
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
Architecture was the most common concern, appearing in 238 Claude.md files, or 72.6% of the dataset.

## Source Excerpt

"these concerns and practices are related to the architecture of the software to be generated with the support of AI agents (found in 238 Claude.md files from our dataset, 72.6%)"

## Note

This is the highest reported frequency among the listed concern categories.
```

### file: evidence-12.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-029
evidence_id: evidence-12
kind: result
citation: Santos et al., arXiv 2511.09268, Sec. 4
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2511.09268
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
General development guidelines appeared in 147 files (44.8%), and project overview appeared in 128 files (39.0%).

## Source Excerpt

"followed by general development guidelines (147 Claude.md files, 44.8%), project overview (128 Claude.md files, 39%)"

## Note

These frequencies are below architecture but above testing and commands in the reported ranking.
```

### file: evidence-13.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-029
evidence_id: evidence-13
kind: result
citation: Santos et al., arXiv 2511.09268, Sec. 4
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2511.09268
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
Testing guidelines appeared in 116 files (35.4%), and commands appeared in 109 files (33.2%).

## Source Excerpt

"and testing guidelines (116 Claude.md files, 35.4%) and commands (109 Claude.md files, 33.2%)."

## Note

The source reports these as recurring themes after architecture, development guidelines, and project overview.
```

### file: evidence-14.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-029
evidence_id: evidence-14
kind: result
citation: Santos et al., arXiv 2511.09268, Sec. 5, Table 2
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2511.09268
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
Code examples were most frequent in Development Guidelines sections at 17.68% of instances in that category.

## Source Excerpt

"As shown, the highest percentage of code examples appears in the Development Guidelines category (17.68% of the instances classified in this category)."

## Note

The source frames this as the highest code-example rate among the listed categories.
```

### file: evidence-15.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-029
evidence_id: evidence-15
kind: result
citation: Santos et al., arXiv 2511.09268, Sec. 5, Table 2
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2511.09268
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
Links were most frequent in Architecture sections at 1.83%, and the authors found only two Mermaid UML diagram instances.

## Source Excerpt

"Conversely, links were most frequently found in the Architecture category, though in only 1.83% of the files... Finally, we also searched for diagrams in the files—specifically UML diagrams written in the syntax of the Mermaid tool, but found only two instances."

## Note

The source presents both links and Mermaid diagrams as relatively rare compared with section text.
```

### file: evidence-16.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-029
evidence_id: evidence-16
kind: result
citation: Santos et al., arXiv 2511.09268, Sec. 6, Table 3
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2511.09268
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The most common Claude.md pattern was Architecture, Dependencies, and Project Overview, appearing in 21.6% of files.

## Source Excerpt

"The most common one includes practices on Architecture, Dependencies, and Project Overview, appearing in 21.6% of the files"

## Note

This result comes from the FP-Max co-occurrence analysis of concern combinations.
```

### file: evidence-17.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-029
evidence_id: evidence-17
kind: incident
citation: Santos et al., arXiv 2511.09268, Sec. 7
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2511.09268
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: bounded
contradiction_relevance: tension
---
The study reports two threats to validity: one-author initial classification and the possibility that results may change as AI agents evolve.

## Source Excerpt

"First, the classification of concerns and practices specified in the Claude.md files was performed by only one author, although it was reviewed and confirmed by two additional authors. Second, the field of AI agents is highly dynamic, meaning that our results may evolve with advances in language models and agent-based tools."

## Note

This caveat limits how strongly the current category counts and patterns should be treated as stable or fully observer-independent.
---

# Extractor Receipt

## Summary

This source contains empirical evidence about how public Claude Code configuration files are collected, filtered, analyzed, and patterned, plus measured frequencies for common configuration concerns, auxiliary elements such as code examples and links, and stated threats to validity.

## Evidence Files

### file: evidence-01.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-029

The initial GitHub Search API sweep found 4,724 repositories containing Claude.md between August 28 and August 30, 2025.

"We first collected an initial list of GitHub repositories which are using Claude Code between August 28, 2025 and August 30, 2025... This initial search returned 4,724 repositories."

### file: evidence-03.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-029
evidence_id: evidence-03
kind: method
citation: Santos et al., arXiv 2511.09268, Sec. 3.1
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2511.09268
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
Repository selection excluded projects with fewer than 100 stars, non-English repositories, and repositories judged not to be real-world applications, then kept the top 100 most popular remaining repositories.

## Source Excerpt

"We removed repositories based on the following criteria: (1) Popularity. We filtered out repositories with less than 100 stars... (2) Language. We excluded non-English repositories... (3) Actual Applications. We removed repositories that are not real-world applications... From the remaining ones, we selected the top-100 most popular repositories"

## Note

The real-world application filter was applied manually from repository description and README content.
```

The selected projects spanned 23 programming languages, with JavaScript/TypeScript in 35 projects, Python in 16, and Go in 9.

"Overall, the projects are implemented in 23 programming languages, with JavaScript/TypeScript (35 projects), Python (16 projects), and Go (9 projects) being the most common ones."

The source reports language counts for projects, not for individual configuration files.

```markdown
---

evidence_id: evidence-05

contradiction_relevance: none
---
The selected repositories had a median of 950 stars, 488 commits, and 58 months since first commit.

## Source Excerpt

"All projects are fairly popular (median of 950 stars) and active (median of 488 commits). They are also long-lived, with a median age of 58 months since their first commit."

## Note

These are median repository-level characteristics for the selected sample.
```

### file: evidence-06.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-029
evidence_id: evidence-06
kind: method
citation: Santos et al., arXiv 2511.09268, Sec. 3.1
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2511.09268
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
During file fetching, the authors found 45 memory-bank files that only pointed to other markdown files and replaced them with the referenced contents, yielding 328 Claude.md files in total.

## Source Excerpt

"During this process, we detected 45 files functioning as memory banks, i.e., files that only pointed to other markdown files within their respective projects. In such cases, we retrieved the contents of the referenced files instead. At the end of this process, we obtained a total of 328 CLAUDE.md files."

## Note

The source treats pointed-to markdown as the analyzable content when a Claude.md file acts only as a pointer.
```

### file: evidence-07.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-029
evidence_id: evidence-07
kind: metric
citation: Santos et al., arXiv 2511.09268, Sec. 3.2, Table 1
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2511.09268
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
Claude.md files had a median of seven level-2 headings, a maximum of 213, and 36 files had no level-2 sections.

## Source Excerpt

"As we can see, the files have a median of seven level-2 headings. However, there is also one file with 213 headings. Interestingly, we found that 36 files have no level-2 sections at all."

## Note

The file-size proxy here is the number of markdown level-2 sections.
```

### file: evidence-08.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-029
evidence_id: evidence-08
kind: method
citation: Santos et al., arXiv 2511.09268, Sec. 3.2
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2511.09268
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
For files without level-2 sections, the authors analyzed level-1 headings instead.

## Source Excerpt

"After a manual analysis, we concluded that these files describe practices and rules directly at level 1 (# tag). Thus, in such cases, we decided to analyze the content of level-1 headings instead."

## Note

This is an explicit exception to the default focus on level-2 headings.
```

### file: evidence-09.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-029
evidence_id: evidence-09
kind: method
citation: Santos et al., arXiv 2511.09268, Sec. 3.2
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2511.09268
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The authors extracted 2,492 section titles and grouped them into semantically related categories through manual analysis by one author, then reviewed the classification with two other authors.

## Source Excerpt

"After following the previous procedures, we extracted 2,492 section titles. These sections were then manually analyzed by one of the authors, who grouped them into semantically related categories... Next, a meeting was held with two other authors, who inspected the proposed classification and confirmed it."

## Note

The categorization process combined single-author coding with multi-author review.
```

### file: evidence-10.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-029
evidence_id: evidence-10

source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2511.09268
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
Pattern detection used the FP-Max algorithm from MLxtend with minimum support set to 0.15.

## Source Excerpt

"We used the FP-Max algorithm to detect which concerns and practices are mentioned together... We used the FP-Max implementation provided by the MLxtend library... configured with 0.15 minimum support."

## Note

Each Claude.md file was represented as a transaction of the concerns and practices it contains.
```

---

```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-029
evidence_id: evidence-17
kind: incident
citation: Santos et al., arXiv 2511.09268, Sec. 7
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2511.09268
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: bounded
contradiction_relevance: tension
---
The study reports two threats to validity: one-author initial classification and the possibility that results may change as AI agents evolve.

## Source Excerpt

"First, the classification of concerns and practices specified in the Claude.md files was performed by only one author, although it was reviewed and confirmed by two additional authors. Second, the field of AI agents is highly dynamic, meaning that our results may evolve with advances in language models and agent-based tools."

## Note

This caveat limits how strongly the current category counts and patterns should be treated as stable or fully observer-independent.
